title: 2nd-order gradient through a NAMED-function inner gradient returns 0 (only inline lambdas work)
goal: Residual gap after ESH-0070/#84 (merged). 2nd-order gradient works when the inner gradient's function arg is an inline lambda, but returns 0 when it's a NAMED function (a var). (gradient (lambda(y)(gradient (lambda(z)(L z)) y)) 3.0)=16 CORRECT; (gradient (lambda(y)(gradient L y)) 3.0)=0 WRONG; (define gL (lambda(x)(gradient (lambda(z)(L z)) x))) (gradient gL 3.0)=0 WRONG. The forward-mode 4-jet + runtime __ad_pert_level path (from #84) fires for inline lambdas but falls back to the old path when the gradient's function arg is a VAR.
fix_hint: Make the forward-4-jet/__ad_pert_level path resolve a VAR function-arg to its lambda body and treat it identically to an inline lambda (same seeding, level push/pop, scalar-body gate). Noesis uses inline lambdas so they're unblocked; this is robustness.
acceptance: (gradient (lambda(y)(gradient L y)) 3.0)=16; (gradient gL 3.0)=16; 50k-iter loop accumulates correctly, no crash; -r AND AOT; nested_ad_loop_test + nested_ad_test 10/10 + vm_ad 12/12 still pass
campaign_payoff: Completes nested AD for the natural (gradient named-fn ...) form.
status_note: MERGED #95 / e6130ecf021f82b6f8995d9d954737b59b1821d8. Swarm worker fixed the runtime scalar nested-gradient named/VAR path in lib/backend/autodiff_codegen.cpp and extended tests/v1_2_edge_cases/dual_scalar_compare_test.esk. Verification: ICC ready/100 before and after, cmake --build build --target eshkol-run, build/eshkol-run -r tests/v1_2_edge_cases/dual_scalar_compare_test.esk -Lbuild => 47 passed, 0 failed, and CI 14/14 SUCCESS.
